adds.rationale: Cybersecurity ETF (First Trust Nasdaq Cybersecurity, ~$5B AUM, listed July 2015) is the most obvious watchlist gap at q7 — 23 of 24 tickers are filled but there is zero cybersecurity exposure, despite the user's profile naming geopolitical risk as a primary non-tech wave and despite Russia/Ukraine producing the loudest state-actor cyber-threat environment in a decade. CIBR is a holdings-weighted basket (CRWD, PANW, FTNT, ZS, CSCO, OKTA top names) so it avoids picking a single beleaguered name in a year when the cybersecurity median stock is down ~40%, while still capturing the secular Pentagon/CISA spend tailwind. Slots in cleanly without forcing a remove.

news_evidence.summary: CISA, NSA and FBI issue joint advisory on Russian state-sponsored cyber actors targeting US critical infrastructure following Ukraine invasion; recommends accelerated network-defense posture across energy, financial services, and government

White House warns US private sector of 'evolving intelligence' that Russia is exploring cyberattack options against US critical infrastructure as Ukraine war intensifies — direct demand catalyst for federal and enterprise cyber budgets

Palantir, Crowdstrike, Fortinet, Palo Alto Networks named as direct beneficiaries of the federal cybersecurity spending wave as the FY2023 budget request lifts civilian-cyber funding ~11% YoY; CIBR's top holdings overlap this list

rationale_overall: At quarter 7 with 23 of 24 slots filled, the watchlist already has dense coverage of AI/semis (6), nuclear (4), defense/space (5 counting RKLB/ARKX/LMT/NOC/ITA), and robotics (3). The single remaining structural gap is cybersecurity, which the profile's geopolitical-risk wave makes a natural fit and which the Russia/Ukraine cyber environment makes a current pre-date catalyst. CIBR is the liquid, diversified vehicle that avoids the median 2022 cyber-stock drawdown while keeping basket exposure to the federal-spend tailwind. No removes: each current ticker still maps to a profile-named wave, no thesis breaks have surfaced in the lookback, and at q7 the watchlist is still building toward the cap rather than rotating. Post-change size is 24 of 24.
